mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns. This signal is always active, reflecting persistent consumption strength.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of months when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, labor market slack reduces wage cost pressures on corporate margins, supporting equity valuations. The signal activates only after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Over a 5-year walk-forward, 20-day returns following UNRATE > 5.5% are negative in at least 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Combined strength in GDP growth and housing starts signals a self-reinforcing economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and equity prices over a quarterly horizon. The interaction of both proxies is required for the signal.
failure_condition: Cumulative 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered quarters over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during the early phase of a recovery signals improving factory output and business investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism is most powerful in early-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: In periods identified as early-cycle, forward 20-day returns are positive less than 60% of the time when INDPRO month-on-month growth exceeds 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect labor market strength and growing consumer spending, which supports corporate revenues and equity prices. This always-on signal is based on the level of payrolls.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS month-on-month growth exceeds 0.2%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in less than 60% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230, it signals elevated inflation that pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term returns within a week.
failure_condition: Over a 2-year walk-forward, 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 230.

mechanism_narrative: Very low federal funds rates combined with a low unemployment rate create a 'Goldilocks' macro environment of easy financial conditions and a strong labor market, boosting equities. The joint condition must hold.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary and tight labor market
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1100 thousand units indicate a residential investment upturn that historically leads equity markets by several months. The signal is regime-dependent, active only during housing market recoveries.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1100, forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of cases over a 10-year walk-forward period.
